title: Wall Street to Procter & Gamble, Colgate-Palmolive: Start Moving Units
text: Investors are no longer happy with big price increases paired with volume declines.

title: Colgate (CL) Q2 Earnings & Sales Surpass Estimates, View Up
text: Colgate-Palmolive's (CL) second-quarter 2023 organic sales advance 8%, with improvements in all divisions and categories.

title: Colgate to keep raising prices as inflation slows to boost margins and profit
text: Colgate-Palmolive Co.'s laser focus on improving margins and strengthening profit appeared to pay off, as continued price increases helped the consumer-products company beat earnings expectations and raise its full-year outlook.

title: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Q2 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: Although the revenue and EPS for Colgate-Palmolive (CL) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Top Estimates
text: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.77 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.75 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.72 per share a year ago.

title: Colgate-Palmolive cleans up with 2Q earnings beat
text: Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) has reported second-quarter earnings that beat expectations as it benefited from price increases and maintained its leadership positions for toothpaste and manual toothbrushes.  The company said its leadership in the toothpaste category continued, with its global market share at 40.5% year to date.

title: Colgate-Palmolive beats earnings forecasts as 11% jump in pricing offsets volume decline
text: Shares of Colgate-Palmolive Co. CL, -1.09% rose 0.9% in premarket trading Friday after the home, personal, oral and pet care products company reported second-quarter profit and revenue that beat expectations and raised its full-year outlook, as another jump in pricing helped offset a volume decline. Net income fell to $502 million, or 60 cents a share, from $603 million, or 72 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Colgate Announces 2nd Quarter 2023 Results
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL): Net sales increased 7.5%; Organic sales* increased 8.0% with growth in every division and in all four categories GAAP EPS declined 17% to $0.60; Base Business EPS* increased 7% to $0.77 GAAP Gross profit margin and Base Business Gross profit margin* both increased 80 basis points to 57.8%, including a negative 70 basis point impact from private label sales resulting from the previously disclosed acquisitions of pet food businesses.

title: Top-Rated Stocks to Watch as Earnings Approach
text: Investors should pay attention to other sectors outside of Energy on Friday as there appears to be opportunity in these Consumer Staples and Auto sector stocks.

title: Should You Buy Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Ahead of Earnings?
text: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) is seeing favorable earnings estimate revision activity and has a positive Zacks Earnings ESP heading into earnings season.

title: Colgate (CL) to Report Q2 Earnings: What's in the Offing?
text: Colgate's (CL) second-quarter 2023 sales are likely to have benefited from strong demand for its products, innovation, brand strength and a focus on premiumization. Rising costs and expenses might have hurt its margins.

title: Our Dividend Growth Portfolio Continues To Outpace The S&P 500
text: In 2015, I purchased 15 of the largest cap U.S. dividend achievers, which have continued to outpace the S&P 500. The portfolio was designed to provide greater protection during recessions and severe bear markets, and began to pull away from the S&P 500 after the world changed in 2020. The portfolio is 55% U.S. stocks and 45% Canadian stocks, with modest allocations to bonds, cash, gold and bitcoin, and a few thematic plays in the electric vehicle and battery space.

title: Oil buoyed ahead of U.S. supply data
text: Oil futures were up modestly early Thursday, attempting to put together back-to-back gains as investors await official data on U.S. crude inventories, with bulls encouraged by expectations the Federal Reserve interest rate-hike cycle is nearing its end.

title: Oil edges higher after back-to-back declines
text: Oil futures rose modestly early Tuesday, stabilizing after back-to-back declines as investors await U.S. supply data.

title: 7 Safe Stocks to Buy as Fed Gears Up for Disinflation
text: While the Federal Reserve aggressively raised the benchmark interest rate to tame historically high inflation, much evidence indicates that the central bank hasn't done enough, thus bolstering the case for safe stocks for investing during disinflation. Fundamentally, the Fed's goal will be to facilitate a gentle deceleration of prices without sparking a recession.

title: Colgate-Palmolive Webcasts 2023 Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call July 28, 2023 – 8:00 a.m. ET
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) will provide a live webcast of its 2023 second quarter earnings conference call on Friday, July 28, 2023, at 8:00 a.m. ET. The call will be hosted by Chairman, President and CEO, Noel Wallace, Chief Financial Officer, Stan Sutula, and Chief Investor Relations Officer and SVP, M&A, John Faucher. At approximately 7:00 a.m. ET, the Company will post its prepared materials (in PDF format) regarding its 2023 second quarter results to.

title: 3 Stocks to Buy for Decreased Volatility
text: With CPI looming, investors may want to visit low-beta stocks as a shield against volatility. As we all know by now, CPI days have become quite a market-moving event.

title: Colgate-Palmolive: Challenging Growth Prospects
text: Colgate-Palmolive's growth has been marginal in recent years due to stiff competition and a saturated market. The company's focus on premiumization and growth in emerging markets, particularly India, may not be enough to significantly boost annual growth rates. Pet Nutrition, though fast-growing, is still a relatively small business and not likely to generate exciting overall growth.

title: Brent oil price pulls back after weak China data
text: The global oil benchmark price fell Wednesday, giving back a chunk of the previous session's gain, after a weak reading on service-sector activity in China, one of the world's largest consumers of crude.

title: Why You Must Consider Colgate (CL) Stock Before Q2 Earnings
text: Colgate (CL) is well-poised on its innovation efforts, pricing actions, digital capabilities and progress on revenue growth management initiatives.

title: 7 Stocks to Buy Now if You Still Think a Recession Is Ahead
text: Preparing for a recession at this juncture seems a ridiculous concept at this juncture. As CNBC pointed out late last week, the Federal Reserve's key inflation measure showed that prices increased by just 0.3% in May, favorably below expectations.

title: Is Colgate-Palmolive's Dividend Safe?
text: Growth isn't what it seems at Colgate-Palmolive.
